text: d12 Deck Two (Whisper Cards) 01 Scheme or Experience extreme déjà vu 02 Check for danger or Seek out normality 03 Arm yourself or Narrate your thoughts 04 Hide something or Make an accusation 05 Seek out food or Get in someone’s face 06 Oppose or Make an accusation 07 Your body has been fully assimilated. You've been REPLACED. Lead the party deeper underground or put people in danger. 08 Be cautious or Show who is in charge 09 Seek shelter or Coordinate 10 Reject or Forge an alliance 11 Demand proof or Seek out normality 12 Break something or Seek shelter Approaching The Portal (p. 27) card: As the party approaches the Portal, place this card in the center of the table. ▶ If you have not been REPLACED: You feel an immense sense of dread and wrongness when looking upon this portal. ▶ If you have been REPLACED: Transform and protect the portal. Quick Reference // A one-stop shop. [39] QUICK REFERENCE HAVE THEY BEEN REPLACED? To determine if an NPC has been REPLACED: ▶Roll a d10 against the DOOM level and make a CTRL save. � If the result is equal or under AND they fail a CTRL save, they have been REPLACED. ▶As the adventure progresses, NPCs’ CTRL may decrease. NPC REACTIONS When the Investigators encounter an NPC whose demeanor would not be obvious, roll 2d6 and consult the tables below. ▶2-4: Hostile ▶5-8: Wary/Paranoid ▶9-10: Curious ▶11: Kind ▶12: Helpful GENERIC NPC STATS STR: 10 DEX: 10 CTRL: 10 HP: 3 Fist (d4) or Improvised Weapon (d6) CHILD OF AMMON (p. 9) STR: 14 DEX: 13 CTRL 10 HP: 8 Armor: 1 ▶Damage: d6 - d12 (number of appendages, size, bones, fungus). ▶ Metamorphosis: Can change forms in moments, sometimes even breaking off into multiple unique instances. ▶ Anointed Blood: Spray a mix of blood, and spores. Witnesses take d4 Stress. ▶ Critical Damage: Fleshy tendrils pierce the victim’s flesh, worming their way between muscle and bone (D4 damage to DEX). DOOM CLOCK (p. 2) D1: Save the mall raffle. D2: Mall phases out. Monster attack. D3: Barrier has everyone trapped. D4: Factions emerge. D5: Child of Ammon caught eating. D6: Power goes out. D7: Supplies sabotaged. Fleshy mass- of-pets escapes pet shop. D8: Water scarsity. D9: Fungal Fields bloom throughout. D10: Mall phases back. PERSONNEL 1. Mr. Neil - Mall Manager (p. 6) 2. Billy - direct report to Mr. Neil (p. 6) 3. Kurt - Stan’s Pretzel Stand (p. 8) 4. Maggie - Head of the CBB (p. 6) 5. Griffith - Your Body is a Temple (p. 7) 6. Samuel - Griffith’s right hand (p. 7) 7. William- Head mall walker (p. 6) 8. Bruce - Maintenance (p. 8) 9. Terry - Night Security (p. 8) 10. Hudson - Head of Security (p. 7) 11. Beth - Head of the Mall Rats (p. 7) 12. Ruth - Patron (p. 7) 13. Emmy - Emmy’s Swords & Stuff (p. 8) 14. Logan - DMV Express (p. 8) Whispers Cards (p. 37) Secret prompts triggered by character action (including every Fallout and often replacing Stress). A few cards cause a PC to become Replaced. Nightmare & Prophecies (p. 31) To sleep is to dream. All PCs make CTRL saves when sleeping. Failing means rolling on the Nightmare Table. APPENDIX N Carpenter, John. - The Thing Astron-6 - The Void Siebert, Maggie - Bonding Umezz, Kazuo - The Drifting Classroom THE MALL The Mall is a wet, gooey, practical effects filled TTRPG adventure set in a 1990s mall pulled out of time and space. The mall’s denizens are trapped inside with a creature not bound by any one form. It slithers among them now, preparing to assimilate and imitate its victims until no one is left and it can open a rift between its home dimension...AND EARTH! Content Warning: This module carries content warnings for death, human sacrifice, hallucinations, drugs, people trapped/imprisoned, instances of self-inflicted harm, temporal disorientation and body horror (bones and foreign objects breaking through skin, unwilling bodily transformation, bodily disfigurement). GOBLIN ARCHIVES’ PLACEHOLDER GOBLIN ARCHIVES
